source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Frontend
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

As one of our first front-end engineers, you’ll ship new features.

What you’ll do:

 - Lead the design and development of core frontend architecture of new products from the ground up.

 - Rapidly prototype and iterate on features to validate ideas and user experience.

 - Establish best practices for code quality, testing, performance, and maintainability.

 - Collaborate closely with design, product, and backend teams to shape the user experience end-to-end.

 - Contribute to key technical and product decisions as part of the founding engineering team.

 - Mentor future engineers and help build a high-caliber frontend team and culture.

Must-have experience:

 - Shipped applications with significant real-world traffic or user counts.

 - Experience leading technical initiatives and shipping scalable, high-performance web and desktop applications.

 - Strong expertise with modern front-end tooling. (TypeScript, CI/CD pipelines)

 - Expertise in optimizing complex UX interactions in web and desktop applications.

 - A passion for creating fast, accessible, and beautiful user experience.

 - Startup mindset: you thrive in fast-moving environments, take ownership, and love solving problems at scale.
   
   


ABOUT COMFY

Comfy (https://www.comfy.org) is the AI creation engine for visual professionals who demand control over every model, every parameter, and every output. The most powerful workflow engine for visual AI.

Unlike tools that hide everything behind a prompt box, Comfy lets you connect models, processing steps, and outputs on a canvas where every decision is visible and every step is inspectable. It gives you the building blocks to create workflows nobody's imagined yet, and share them with everyone.

What started as an open-source project in 2023 now has 4 million users, 60,000+ community-built nodes, and 150,000+ daily downloads. It's used by artists, filmmakers, game studios, designers, researchers, and VFX houses, including teams at OpenAI, Netflix, Amazon Studios, Ubisoft, EA, and Tencent.

We're a small, intense team in San Francisco. Our team comes from Stability AI and Google, and many contributed to the ComfyUI ecosystem long before working here. Low ego, high ownership. We work hard and demand a lot of each other, but we have fun. Everyone here is building something meaningful that will end up being our life's work.

If this mission excites you and you view yourself as top-tier talent, your future latent self is waiting for you at Comfy.

Check out our Github (https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI) and blog (https://blog.comfy.org). We recently raised $30M at a $500M valuation. The round was led by Craft, with participation from Pace Capital, Chemistry, TruArrow, and others.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Backend Generalist
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

You’ll lead critical backend initiatives for the millions of creatives using ComfyUI, building systems that run millions of AI workflows. This means building rock solid distributed systems, designing data models that support explosive growth, and solving difficult scaling and optimization problems. You will make code changes across open source ComfyUI and Comfy Cloud. Our stack is Python, Go, Kubernetes, and Postgres on the backend, but we care way more about your ability to learn and ship than whether you've used these exact languages before.

You might be a good fit if

 - You enjoy having high levels of ownership to ship high quality features

 - You habitually invent novel solutions to solve hard problems

 - You've build a great product before, and care about the user experience as much as the engineering behind it

 - You have a strong understanding of distributed systems and scaled large systems before

What you'll do

 - Work with a cross-functional team to quickly ship features, often going from idea to production in days not weeks

 - Ship features that directly impact millions of ComfyUI users

 - Help shape our technical direction as we grow

Sample Projects

 - Deploy ComfyUI’s diverse custom nodes written by the open source community in a secure and maintainable way

 - Write algorithms to schedule a diverse and heterogenous set of workflows on GPU nodes, optimizing for both latency and throughput

 - Build a notification system from scratch in one week

Nice to have

 - Are not afraid to deep dive into infrastructure (Kubernetes / Helm) to get things done

If you don't know every piece of our stack, that's fine - we care about your ability to learn fast and ship quality code. We're outpacing the industry by learning what we need to solve problems, not by sticking to what we already know.




ABOUT COMFY

Comfy (https://www.comfy.org) is the AI creation engine for visual professionals who demand control over every model, every parameter, and every output. The most powerful workflow engine for visual AI.

Unlike tools that hide everything behind a prompt box, Comfy lets you connect models, processing steps, and outputs on a canvas where every decision is visible and every step is inspectable. It gives you the building blocks to create workflows nobody's imagined yet, and share them with everyone.

What started as an open-source project in 2023 now has 4 million users, 60,000+ community-built nodes, and 150,000+ daily downloads. It's used by artists, filmmakers, game studios, designers, researchers, and VFX houses, including teams at OpenAI, Netflix, Amazon Studios, Ubisoft, EA, and Tencent.

We're a small, intense team in San Francisco. Our team comes from Stability AI and Google, and many contributed to the ComfyUI ecosystem long before working here. Low ego, high ownership. We work hard and demand a lot of each other, but we have fun. Everyone here is building something meaningful that will end up being our life's work.

If this mission excites you and you view yourself as top-tier talent, your future latent self is waiting for you at Comfy.

Check out our Github (https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI) and blog (https://blog.comfy.org). We recently raised $30M at a $500M valuation. The round was led by Craft, with participation from Pace Capital, Chemistry, TruArrow, and others.

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff ML Engineer, Performance Optimization
source_jobs.description:
The Role

We're looking for someone who loves optimizing model inference to join us in building the core of ComfyUI - the most complex and bleeding-edge part of our engine. You'll be working on making AI models run faster and more efficiently than anyone thought possible.



You are a good fit if this describes you:

 - You geek out about model inference, torch optimizations, and memory management

 - You've written production PyTorch code that pushes performance boundaries

 - You love diving deep into how models actually work under the hood

 - You get excited about making insanely optimized code that just works

 - You think the current state of ML deployment could be way better



What you'll do:

 - Build and optimize the core inference engine that powers ComfyUI

 - Make massive models run faster and use less memory than anyone else

 - Work directly with our core team on architecting new features

 - Tackle the hardest technical problems in the visual AI space

 - Help shape where we take this technology next

Bonus: If you've worked with diffusion/LLM models before or built custom nodes for ComfyUI, that's awesome

source_jobs.title: Freelance Motion Designer
source_jobs.description:
The Role

We’re looking for Freelance Motion Designers to help us show what artists can create with ComfyUI. You’ll work closely with our creative technologists to create world class work at the forefront of the industry. From product demos and community spotlights to high-impact launch films, help us define what the future of creativity will be.



Who We’re Looking For

 - 3–6 years of experience motion design (agency, in-house, or creative studio).

 - Strong portfolio that shows an impeccable sense of taste, pacing, and the ability to tell stories effectively.

 - Proficient in Adobe CC and a 3D package.

 - Highly technical with the ability to learn new tools quickly.

 - Experience working with a high degree of autonomy in fast paced production environments.

 - Comfortable using AI tools as part of your work.



What You’ll Do

 - Edit and animate model release videos, feature demos, and partner showcases that inspire and educate artists.

 - Combine footage, motion graphics, typography, and sound to build engaging narratives.

 - Collaborate closely with the Creative Director to refine video content for our social channels.

 - Create new content using ComfyUI and integrate it into polished motion pieces.

 - Assist in concepting for various marketing campaigns.



About Comfy

source_jobs.title: Creative Artist
source_jobs.description:
The Role

We’re expanding our go-to-market and creative efforts with a team based in San Francisco and looking for a Creative Artist who can define and lead the visual voice of Comfy across model launches, product showcases, and community storytelling.



What You’ll Do

 - Define Comfy’s creative identity for go-to-market — build a unified visual language across video, web, events, and campaigns.

 - Lead end-to-end creative development for video content for feature launches, brand collaborations, etc. Design the initial concept and style frames, and collaborate with video editors & AI artists for final execution.

 - Develop and maintain brand style guides, motion direction, and visual tone for all GTM assets.

 - Stay current with design trends, emerging technologies, and industry best practices to bring fresh, innovative thinking to every project.



You are a good fit if this describes you:

 - Proven experience leading professional creative teams while remaining hands-on in the design process.

 - Portfolio shows brand systems with multi-touch consistency; inventive storytelling and art direction across multiple styles/formats.

 - Deep proficiency in graphic design tools and a solid understanding of video editing workflows.

 - Familiarity with the AI content production pipeline; comfortable prototyping with new models/AI tools; can brief editors and collaborating artists clearly.

 - Exceptional communication, presentation, and leadership skills.

 - Highly organized, proactive, and able to inspire and motivate teams under tight timelines.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Product Designer who thrives in complexity; someone who can take a highly technical creative tool and make it elegant, clear, and joyful to use. You’ll be part of our Product team and work on shaping the next generation of ComfyUI across desktop and cloud.

You’ll own end-to-end design for critical product areas: from early UX flows and wireframes to polished, production-ready UI.

Your work will directly impact how millions of artists and engineers interact with AI.

What You’ll Do

 - Design end-to-end product experiences across Comfy Cloud and desktop, from early UX flows to final polished UI
   
   - Translate deep technical systems into approachable, elegant interactions
   
   - Handle all the “real world” details e.g. empty states, edge cases, loading behaviors, and interaction feedback. Users should never feel lost or clueless on next steps

 - Collaborate closely with engineering to ensure seamless implementation and design fidelity, providing clear specs and design rationale.

 - Apply, maintain, and evolve ComfyUI’s design system, ensuring consistency across components, themes, and interaction patterns

 - Prototype, test, and iterate quickly to validate ideas

 - Contribute to a design culture that values clarity, speed, and first principles thinking

Requirements

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating taste, creativity, and elegant solutions to complex product problems

 - Comfortable working independently, taking rough wireframes or specs to production-ready design

 - Experienced in collaborating with engineers to balance design intent with feasibility

 - Ability to simplify complex technical workflows without sacrificing flexibility or power

 - Fluency in Figma and modern design-system practices

 - Proven experience designing complex and creative digital products (e.g. video or image editors, 3D or VFX, video games, etc)

 - Prior usage of ComfyUI is highly desirable

Salary:

$150,000-300,000 plus equity

Application Details

Apply with your portfolio (required), resume, and a short note on why you want to design for ComfyUI.

If your portfolio can’t be shared publicly, we can review it privately during the interview

This is an in-person role in San Francisco.




ABOUT COMFY

Comfy (https://www.comfy.org) is the AI creation engine for visual professionals who demand control over every model, every parameter, and every output. The most powerful workflow engine for visual AI.

Unlike tools that hide everything behind a prompt box, Comfy lets you connect models, processing steps, and outputs on a canvas where every decision is visible and every step is inspectable. It gives you the building blocks to create workflows nobody's imagined yet, and share them with everyone.

What started as an open-source project in 2023 now has 4 million users, 60,000+ community-built nodes, and 150,000+ daily downloads. It's used by artists, filmmakers, game studios, designers, researchers, and VFX houses, including teams at OpenAI, Netflix, Amazon Studios, Ubisoft, EA, and Tencent.

We're a small, intense team in San Francisco. Our team comes from Stability AI and Google, and many contributed to the ComfyUI ecosystem long before working here. Low ego, high ownership. We work hard and demand a lot of each other, but we have fun. Everyone here is building something meaningful that will end up being our life's work.

If this mission excites you and you view yourself as top-tier talent, your future latent self is waiting for you at Comfy.

Check out our Github (https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI) and blog (https://blog.comfy.org). We recently raised $30M at a $500M valuation. The round was led by Craft, with participation from Pace Capital, Chemistry, TruArrow, and others.

source_jobs.title: Graphic Designer
source_jobs.description:
The Role

At ComfyUI, our tools empower the world’s best creatives to make incredible things. We’re looking for a talented Graphic Designer to help us support and reach that these artists through fantastic design across everything we do. You’ll be working closely with our Creative, Product, and Marketing teams. Your initial focus will be taking ownership of our web presence, maintaining our newly refreshed brand system, and building high-impact brand toolkits. If you are passionate about visual storytelling, love building scalable design templates, and want to create work that inspires other creators, we’d love to have you on the team.



What You’ll Do

 - Maintain brand guidelines as our company and product grows quickly, ensuring visual consistency and quality across all design systems.

 - Design and optimize web experiences, focusing heavily on core pages and campaign landing pages for our website.

 - Execute high-volume production design, including building and scaling out robust template libraries for video thumbnails, social assets, and presentations.

 - Collaborate closely with the Creative Director to elevate the overall visual quality and artistic direction of the brand.

 - Create new content using ComfyUI and integrate it into your designs, utilizing the very tools our community uses.

 - Assist in creative development for product launches, feature updates, and various marketing campaigns.



Who We’re Looking For

 - 3–6 years of design experience in an agency, in-house, or creative studio environment.

 - A stellar portfolio that demonstrates an impeccable sense of taste and versatile work across multiple channels (web, presentation design, social media, etc.).

 - A strong design perspective with a mastery of color, typography, and layout.

 - Technical proficiency in Adobe Creative Cloud and Figma.

 - A systems thinker with proven experience building out and managing brand template libraries in Figma.

 - A highly technical, quick learner who thrives with a high degree of autonomy in fast-paced production environments.

 - Comfortable using AI tools as a core part of your daily workflow.



About Comfy

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, ComfyUI
source_jobs.description:
You will work closely with design, engineering, and AI to deliver a world-class experience for users of ComfyUI. You will be trusted to envision the future of creative work in the age of AI, and how ComfyUI can help creators make their creative vision come true.

The ideal candidate will be familiar with ComfyUI, have a demonstrated passionate for creative work, and highly technical.

ComfyUI is one of the most active AI projects on GitHub with 100k+ Github stars, thousands of issues, hundreds of pull requests, and a passionate community pushing the boundaries of what's possible. We work with the best creators in the world, from Hollywood directors to model research labs.

What you'll do

 - Develop a roadmap for ComfyUI (including Comfy Cloud) in collaboration with engineering and product design leads

 - Anticipate user needs and opportunities for innovation and value

 - Stay on top of both qualitative and quantitative user feedback to understand how to make strong data-driven decisions.

 - Have conviction to make difficult product decisions that balance performance, usability, familiarity, and innovation.

 - Come up with creative new ways to make ComfyUI the best tool for serious creatives.

 - Develop agentic user experiences for ComfyUI or within ComfyUI

You might be a fit if…

 - You know ComfyUI deeply — not just as a user, but you understand how it works under the hood

 - Has worked on software used by professionals before — doesn't need to be in the creative field

 - Highly technical with demonstrated coding experience in the past, either as a software engineer or with equivalent computer science background

 - Can build vibe-coded prototypes and dig into bugs in code.

 - Strong experience with product management in small, rapid-paced teams.

 - You've shipped something awesome before for an extended period of time




ABOUT COMFY

Unlike tools that hide everything behind a prompt box, Comfy lets you connect models, processing steps, and outputs on a canvas where every decision is visible and every step is inspectable. It gives you the building blocks to create workflows nobody's imagined yet, and share them with everyone.

What started as an open-source project in 2023 now has 4 million users, 60,000+ community-built nodes, and 150,000+ daily downloads. It's used by artists, filmmakers, game studios, designers, researchers, and VFX houses, including teams at OpenAI, Netflix, Amazon Studios, Ubisoft, EA, and Tencent.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Creative Technologist
source_jobs.description:
About the role

The Forward Deployed Creative Technologist sits at the front line of every customer engagement we run. When a Fortune 500 brand team, a major studio, or a fast-growing agency wants to see what Comfy can do with their data, their style, their pipeline, you’re the one who builds it. Then you ride along through close and into production. Part Comfy expert, part GTM, part dev rel. You’ll work directly with our Founding Account Executive and the broader GTM team.

The depth we expect on Comfy itself is the bar. The bar isn’t “I’ve used it.” The bar is “I’ve shipped output I’m proud of, broken things, fixed them, and have opinions on workflows.”



What you’ll do

 - Build with customers. Custom workflows, demo nodes, proof-of-concept pipelines that close deals. When a prospect asks “can Comfy do this?” you have a working answer within days.

 - Run technical discovery on sales calls. Translate customer problems into Comfy solutions in real time. Spot what’s possible, what’s not, what’s worth scoping, and what’s a trap.

 - Own the implementation handoff. Once we close, you ride along until the workflow is shipping in production. Not a permanent CSM role, but you don’t disappear after the contract is signed.

 - Create content that compounds. Demo videos, workflow recipes, tutorials, community posts. Some of it is sales enablement. Some of it is dev rel. The best of it is both.

 - Be the bridge to engineering and design. Customer feedback lands with you first. You’ll work with our PMs and engineers to turn the sharpest signal into roadmap input.
   
   

You might be a good fit if

 - You’ve used ComfyUI seriously. Not “I tried it once.” You’ve built workflows, broken them, fixed them, and shipped output you’re proud of. We’ll ask to see it.

 - You have creative range across image and video.

 - You can write Python and read other people’s code. You don’t need to be an engineer. You need to be dangerous enough to build custom nodes and debug other people’s workflows.

 - You’re a sharp listener, fast learner, calm under pressure, comfortable in front of customers.

 - You move fast and ship. You’d rather show than tell.

 - You hold your own in a room with senior creative directors, ML engineers, and procurement at the same Zoom.
   
   

Bonus if you have

 - Experience at a creative agency, studio, or in-house brand team (MediaMonks, R/GA, Active Theory, etc.)

 - A public portfolio, GitHub, or social presence built around AI-generated work.

 - Open-source contributions to ComfyUI or adjacent projects.

 - Prior exposure to B2B SaaS, even informally (founder, early-stage anything, freelance client work).
   
   

Not a fit if

 - You’re a sales engineer looking for the next sales engineer seat. This role is more creative, more hands-on, and less scripted than that.

 - You want to be a pure builder. Half of this job is in front of customers.

 - You need a manager to tell you what to build next. You’ll be expected to read the deal and choose the build.

 - You bristle at direct feedback. Our cycle is fast and our notes are direct.
   
   

Applying

Send your resume and a link to a ComfyUI workflow you’ve built. Tell us what problem it solves and what you’d change about it. If you don’t have one, build one and send it.

Location: San Francisco, in-office. Occasional travel (a handful of times per year) for on-site deployment at strategic enterprise accounts.



About Comfy

Comfy (https://www.comfy.org) is the AI creation engine for visual professionals who demand control over every model, every parameter, and every output. The most powerful workflow engine for visual AI.

Unlike tools that hide everything behind a prompt box, Comfy lets you connect models, processing steps, and outputs on a canvas where every decision is visible and every step is inspectable. It gives you the building blocks to create workflows nobody's imagined yet, and share them with everyone.

What started as an open-source project in 2023

source_jobs.title: Account Executive: SMB & Mid-Market
source_jobs.description:
About the role

You’ll own our SMB and mid-market motion: studios, agencies, in-house brand teams, indie shops, and the long tail of fast-moving creative teams that are already in love with ComfyUI and ready to put it on a credit card or a procurement form. You’ll report to our Founding AE and you’ll have real inbound demand from day one.

The motion is high velocity, sometimes transactional, often times consultative. You’ll need to read each deal and choose the right play.

We’re explicitly open on background. The ideal candidate has 1-3 years of B2B SaaS AE experience. We’re equally open to someone from inside the creative industry who has the raw material to sell and wants to learn the craft.



What you’ll do

 - Run the full sales cycle. Inbound leads, outbound prospecting, discovery, demo, negotiation, close. You own the pipeline end to end.

 - Sell to creative teams. Studios, agencies, in-house brand teams, indie shops. The buyers are often technical, sometimes not. You’ll need to shift between the two.

 - Help build the motion as you go. SMB and mid-market playbooks at Comfy are still being written. You’ll test, share what works, and help shape how we sell at this segment.

 - Ramp deep on the product. ComfyUI is a real piece of software with real depth. You won’t be selling buzzwords. The best AEs here will know the product deeply within 60 days.

 - Feed customer signal back into the org. What’s resonating, what’s blocking, what customers wish existed.



You might be a good fit if

 - You’ve sold B2B SaaS before, or you’re a strong SDR/BDR ready to graduate. We care about deal evidence, not titles. Show us cycles you ran and deals you closed.

 - You can hold your own in a creative conversation. You don’t need to be a ComfyUI power user, but you should be genuinely curious about the work our customers do.

 - You’re sharp on the phone and on email. Quick listener. You can run a discovery call that surfaces what’s actually going on, not a checklist of qualification questions.

 - You can work without a playbook. Most of what you do this year won’t have a template. You’ll make calls, document what worked, and keep moving.

 - You’re honest about what’s working and what isn’t. We don’t want a forecast that looks good. We want a forecast that’s right.



Bonus if you have

 - Experience selling AI, ML, dev tools, or creative SaaS.

 - Background in the creative industry before moving to sales (agency, studio, in-house brand team).

 - Comfort with technical buyers (engineers, technical artists, ML practitioners).

 - Familiarity with ComfyUI or adjacent generative AI tools.



Not a fit if

 - Your sales motion is rooted in scripts and cadences and you can’t operate without them.

 - You’re looking for a brand-name logo on your resume rather than the work itself.

 - You bristle at direct feedback. Our cycle is fast and our notes are direct.

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff ML Engineer, Model Integration
source_jobs.description:
The Role

We're looking for someone who lives on the frontier of generative AI to join us in building the core of ComfyUI. You'll be the person who takes the newest open-source models (image, video, 3D, audio, multimodal…) and brings them into ComfyUI as first-class citizens, often within days of their release.



You are a good fit if this describes you:

 - You read new model papers as they drop and have opinions about them

 - You love diving deep into how models actually work under the hood

 - You've ported models from research into production and gotten the outputs to match

 - You know ComfyUI well, its architecture, workflows and have opinions about nodes design

 - You think the gap between research and production should be as short as possible



What you'll do:

 - Implement state-of-the-art open-source models to run natively in the ComfyUI core engine

 - Design and build the native nodes that expose new model capabilities cleanly to users

 - Work directly with our core team on architecting new features

 - Partner with the custom node community and help core absorb capabilities as they mature

 - Help shape where we take this technology next

Bonus: If you've worked with diffusion/LLM models before or built custom nodes for ComfyUI, that's awesome

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About the role

You'll own the full sales cycle for our enterprise accounts: Fortune 500 brand teams, major studios, large agencies, game studios, and VFX houses that want to put Comfy at the center of their production pipeline. These are the accounts where Comfy goes from a tool a few artists love into infrastructure a whole org depends on. You'll inherit warm demand - many of these teams are already running ComfyUI in the wild and want a real commercial relationship.

The motion is consultative and multi-threaded. Six-figure-plus deals, multiple stakeholders, real procurement, security review, and legal. Cycles run months, not days. You'll quarterback each deal end to end: build the business case, line up the right people on our side, and drive it to close - then expand.

You'll own your deals and play a central part in the broader enterprise motion - running the plays, surfacing what's working, and helping the whole effort get sharper as we scale it.

We're looking for someone who has closed enterprise SaaS before and can operate without a finished playbook. The ideal candidate has 5+ years of B2B SaaS AE experience with a track record of landing and growing six-figure accounts. We're also open to a strong mid-market closer ready to move up-market who has the raw material and wants the bigger game.



What you'll do

Own complex, multi-stakeholder deals end to end. Source and develop enterprise opportunities, run discovery, scope and orchestrate proof-of-concept work, build the business case, navigate procurement/security/legal, and close.

Sell to the whole buying committee. Creative directors, technical artists, ML engineers, IT/security, and procurement are often in the same deal. You'll tailor the story to each and keep them aligned.

Quarterback the deal. Pull in the right internal partners for technical proof, PMs and engineering for roadmap questions, and leadership for executive sponsorship. You run the deal; you don't do it alone.

Land and expand. The first contract is the beginning. Build account plans that grow seats, workloads, and use cases over time.

Contribute to the enterprise motion. The enterprise playbook at Comfy is still being written. You'll be in the thick of it - testing plays in your own deals, sharing what works, and helping sharpen how we sell at this segment.

Ramp deep on the product. ComfyUI has real depth. You won't be selling buzzwords; you'll be credible with technical buyers and know the product well enough to scope what's real.

Feed customer signal back into the org. What enterprise buyers need, what blocks deals, what they wish existed - that signal shapes the roadmap.



You might be a good fit if

You've closed enterprise B2B SaaS - six-figure-plus deals with long, multi-threaded cycles. We care about deal evidence, not logos. Show us deals you ran and how you ran them.

You can multi-thread an org and build consensus across technical and non-technical buyers, all the way up to executive sponsors.

You're comfortable owning forecast and pipeline rigor on deals that take months and have many moving parts.

You can hold your own in a creative conversation. You don't need to be a ComfyUI power user, but you should be genuinely curious about the work our customers do.

You can work without a playbook. Most of what you do this year won't have a template. You'll make the call, document what worked, and keep moving.

You're honest about what's working and what isn't. We don't want a forecast that looks good. We want a forecast that's right.



Bonus if you have

Experience selling AI, ML, dev tools, or creative SaaS into the enterprise.

Relationships in the creative industry - studios, agencies, in-house brand teams, game/VFX.

Comfort with technical buyers (engineers, technical artists, ML practitioners) and with security/procurement processes at large orgs.

Familiarity with ComfyUI or adjacent generative AI tools.

Experience selling in an early or still-forming enterprise motion, not j

source_jobs.title: Head of Finance
source_jobs.description:
The Role

You'll be finance hire #1 at Comfy.

Comfy is growing fast: 4 million users, a fast-scaling cloud business, and a fresh $30M raise. So far we've run finance out of spreadsheets, outsourced bookkeeping, and founder intuition. That's no longer enough.

As our Head of Finance, you'll build the entire finance function from zero. You'll be the senior-most finance person in the company, reporting directly to the founders, and you'll own everything with a dollar sign attached to it — from the financial model and runway to the next fundraise, from the unit economics of a compute-heavy AI business to payroll, accounting, and controls.

This is a true 0→1 mandate. You won't inherit a team, a model, or a playbook — you'll build them. You'll move between boardroom-level strategy and the operational plumbing in the same afternoon, and you'll bring the financial rigor that lets us make sharp, fast decisions as we scale. Every dollar you help us deploy well compounds into Comfy's trajectory.



What You'll Do

Build the finance function from scratch: Stand up FP&A, accounting, treasury, and controls. Choose the tools, design the processes, and decide what to keep in-house vs. outsource — then run it.

Own the financial model and FP&A: Build and maintain the company model, budget, and forecast. Drive the annual and quarterly planning process, track actuals vs. plan, and turn variances into decisions.

Master a compute-heavy business: Get deep on our unit economics — cost-to-serve, GPU/inference COGS, gross margin, and the levers behind them. Be the person who knows exactly how a usage-based AI business makes (and loses) money.

Manage cash and runway: Own cash flow forecasting, burn, and runway. Give the founders an always-accurate picture of how much time and room we have to maneuver.

Run accounting, close, and controls: Own the monthly close, the books, and the relationship with outsourced accounting/tax partners — and build toward bringing the right pieces in-house as we grow.

Partner on the next raise: Build the board and investor materials, own the data room and diligence, and be a trusted partner to the founders through fundraising and strategic decisions.

Inform pricing and monetization: Work with GTM and Product on pricing, packaging, and the economics of new offerings so growth and margin move together.

Own the critical back-office: Payroll, benefits, equity and cap table management, tax, audit, insurance, and vendor/contract management — the unglamorous things that have to be exactly right.

Build the team: As the function grows, hire and mentor the finance and accounting team behind you.



You Might Be a Good Fit If You

Have 9+ years across strategic finance, FP&A, and/or accounting — including time at a high-growth startup — and have either been the senior-most finance person or a strong #2 ready to step up and own it.

Have built things 0→1: a model, a planning process, or a finance function from a blank page, not just maintained someone else's.

Are an exceptional modeler who can move from a clean three-statement model to a back-of-envelope answer in a Slack thread without losing rigor.

Are equally comfortable with strategy and the operational plumbing — you'll set the financial direction and also reconcile the books.

Understand usage-based / consumption business models and COGS-heavy economics (bonus: AI, infrastructure, or compute).

Are data-literate — comfortable getting your own answers from the data (SQL or BI a strong plus) rather than waiting to be handed a report.

Operate from first principles with high agency and low ego. You bias toward action, communicate proactively, and own outcomes end-to-end.

Are SF-based or willing to relocate to join our in-person rhythm near Alamo Square.



Bonus If You Have

Experience at an open-source, developer-tools, or creator-tools company.

Been a core finance partner through a fundraise (Seed → A → B).

Carried early-stage scrappiness alongside later-stage rigor —

source_jobs.title: Lead, Strategic Finance
source_jobs.description:
The Role

You'll be finance hire #1 at Comfy.

Comfy is growing fast: 4 million users, a fast-scaling cloud business, and a fresh $30M raise. So far we've run finance out of spreadsheets, outsourced bookkeeping, and founder intuition. That's no longer enough.

As our Lead, Strategic Finance, you'll be the first finance hire and the person who builds the analytical backbone of the company. You'll own the financial model, the planning process, and the unit economics of a compute-heavy AI business — and you'll roll up your sleeves on the operational finance basics too.

This is a hands-on, builder's role. You won't inherit a model or a playbook — you'll build them from a blank page. You'll live in the data, turn it into decisions the founders actually use, and bring financial rigor to a team that has been moving on instinct. It's a rare chance to own finance end-to-end early, with a clear path to grow as the function (and your scope) scales beneath you.



What You'll Do

Build the financial model from scratch: Own the company's three-statement model, budget, and forecast. Make it the source of truth the founders rely on for every major decision.

Run FP&A end-to-end: Drive budgeting, forecasting, and the planning cadence. Track actuals vs. plan, explain variances, and translate the numbers into clear recommendations.

Crack the unit economics: Get deep on cost-to-serve, GPU/inference COGS, gross margin, and retention/expansion. Be the person who knows exactly how a usage-based AI business makes and loses money.

Live in the data: Pull your own numbers, build KPI dashboards, and turn messy data into clean insight. You won't wait for someone to hand you a report — you'll build it.

Model the big decisions: Build scenarios for pricing, hiring, compute spend, and new bets so the team can see the financial consequences before committing.

Support fundraising and the board: Help build investor and board materials, own key metrics, and assemble the analysis behind the company's story.

Partner with GTM and Product: Work on pricing, packaging, and monetization so growth and margin move together.

Keep the basics tight: Track cash and runway, and manage the relationship with outsourced accounting/payroll partners so the operational side stays accurate while you build the strategic side.



You Might Be a Good Fit If You

Have 6+ years in strategic finance, FP&A, investment banking, private equity, consulting, or a high-growth startup finance team — with real hands-on ownership, not just slide support.

Are an exceptional modeler who can build a clean three-statement model and a fast back-of-envelope answer with equal rigor.

Are data-fluent — comfortable getting your own answers (SQL or BI a strong plus) rather than waiting to be handed a dataset.

Understand usage-based / consumption business models and COGS-heavy economics (bonus: AI, infrastructure, or compute).

Are scrappy and self-directed — you've built things 0→1 and are happy owning both the strategy and the operational plumbing while the function is small.

Operate from first principles with high agency and low ego. You bias toward action, communicate proactively, and own outcomes end-to-end.

Are San Francisco based or willing to relocate to join our in-person rhythm.



Bonus If You Have

Experience at an open-source, developer-tools, or creator-tools company.

Been part of a fundraise as the person behind the model and the metrics.

Stood up an FP&A or strategic finance function from scratch.

Used Comfy or have real familiarity with AI creative tools.



Compensation

Base salary: $150K–$225K

Equity: + meaningful equity

Plus benefits and relocation support for SF.



About Comfy

Comfy (https://www.comfy.org) is the AI creation engine for visual professionals who demand control over every model, every parameter, and every output. The most powerful workflow engine for visual AI.

Unlike tools that hide everything behind a prompt box, Comfy lets you connect models, processi

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Scientist, Product & Growth
source_jobs.description:
The Role

Comfy is looking for our first dedicated product and growth data scientist. Millions of people use ComfyUI, and we're turning that reach into a real business. We need someone who can tell us what's actually happening inside the funnel, find where the leverage is, and prove what moves the numbers.

This is a hands-on, senior IC role. You'll own the analysis behind signup, activation, conversion, and retention, and you'll work directly with growth, product, and the founders. You won't sit behind a ticket queue. You'll pick the questions that matter, answer them well, and turn the answers into decisions we actually make.



What You'll Do

Own the metrics that define our growth funnel: signup, activation, conversion, retention, and how they connect

Design and run experiments (A/B tests and beyond), and read them honestly, including when the answer is "no effect"

Build the models that matter for growth: activation prediction, churn and retention, conversion propensity, and LTV

Turn messy product and billing events into clean, trusted analysis (we run on PostHog and a warehouse)

Find the leverage: the few places where a small change in the funnel produces an outsized result, and size the opportunity before we build

Make your work legible with clear metrics, dashboards, and writeups the whole team can act on

Partner with growth and product day to day, and with the founders on the bets that matter



You Might Be a Fit If You

Have several years in product or growth data science, ideally at a product-led or consumer company with real scale

Are genuinely hands-on: you write your own SQL and Python, build your own models, and don't need a platform team to get answers

Know experimentation cold, both the statistics and the judgment, and can tell a real effect from noise

Think in funnels and systems, and connect a metric to the decision it should drive

Communicate in plain language, and aren't afraid to deliver an inconvenient finding

Have built data models or pipelines when the job needed it (dbt, warehouse), even if analysis is your main game

Care about AI and creative tools, ideally as a ComfyUI user

Like a small, intense team: high ownership, low ego, fast clock

Are in SF or ready to be



Bonus Points

You've stood up an experimentation program or an analytics stack from scratch

You're comfortable with PostHog, dbt, or similar tools

You can point to analysis that changed what a company did, not just what it knew

Benefits include company-sponsored Medical, Dental, and Vision (up to 100% coverage), unlimited PTO, daily catered lunch and dinner, your choice of equipment, and an unlimited budget for AI tools.



About ComfyUI

The creative world is changing fast. Everything that used to be rendered is being generated by AI. ComfyUI https://www.comfy.org/ is the world's leading visual AI platform, an open, modular system to build AI workflows.

It's used by artists, filmmakers, video game creators, designers, researchers, VFX houses, and among others, teams at OpenAI, Netflix, Amazon Studios, Ubisoft, EA, and Tencent, all who want to go beyond presets and truly shape how AI creates.

Open-source, with over 100k GitHub Stars

An inference engine to run the most powerful open source models locally or in the cloud

A massive community, with thousands of community-made nodes and integrations

Scales from creative experimentation to production automation

Evolving to democratize visual AI creation, empowering everyone from hobbyists to studios, storytellers, and enterprises to be more productive and creative than ever before

ComfyUI isn't just another AI app. It is the operating system for visual generative AI, the foundation on which the next generation of creative tools are built.

notes: Highly technical product with strong engineering focus, but also has a growing go-to-market team.
